snippetA: My cat's name is Juniper.
snippetB: My cat's name is Marlo.
rationale: Both assert different current names for the same pet with no time marker.

snippetA: The Aster project lead is Priya Nair.
snippetB: The Aster project lead is Leo Chen.
rationale: The same role on the same project is assigned to two different people.

snippetA: My dentist is Dr. Rana Velasquez.
snippetB: My dentist is Dr. Omar Sato.
rationale: Two current providers are named for the same role without evidence of an update.

snippetA: The Pinebox server runs on Azure.
snippetB: The Pinebox server runs on Hetzner.
rationale: The hosting platform for the same server is incompatible as stated.

snippetA: My preferred invoice email is billing@lumenforge.test.
snippetB: My preferred invoice email is accounts@lumenforge.test.
rationale: Only one preferred invoice email is implied for the same organization.

snippetA: The Willow Lane apartment allows dogs.
snippetB: The Willow Lane apartment does not allow dogs.
rationale: These directly disagree about the same lease policy.

snippetA: Marta's birthday is April 6.
snippetB: Marta's birthday is September 18.
rationale: A person's birthday cannot normally be both dates.

snippetA: The Nebula vendor contract expires in November.
snippetB: The Nebula vendor contract expires in February.
rationale: The same contract has conflicting expiration months.

snippetA: My emergency contact is Rafi Benes.
snippetB: My emergency contact is Elena Park.
rationale: The same personal contact slot has two different people and no update marker.

snippetA: The Brindle workshop starts at 9 AM.
snippetB: The Brindle workshop starts at 10 AM.
rationale: The start time conflicts unless one was a later reschedule, which is not stated.

snippetA: My home Wi-Fi password is riverlamp42.
snippetB: My home Wi-Fi password is cedarclock42.
rationale: Two different current passwords are asserted for the same network.

snippetA: The Morpho prototype uses PostgreSQL.
snippetB: The Morpho prototype uses SQLite.
rationale: The storage backend for the same prototype is stated differently.

snippetA: I am allergic to cashews.
snippetB: I am not allergic to cashews.
rationale: These are opposite health facts about the same allergy.

snippetA: The Solstice team has 12 members.
snippetB: The Solstice team has 18 members.
rationale: A numeric headcount drift without dates should be adjudicated.

snippetA: My passport is in the blue drawer.
snippetB: My passport is in the hallway safe.
rationale: The current location of the same item is incompatible as saved.

snippetA: Kavi reports to Simone Bell.
snippetB: Kavi reports to Jules Armand.
rationale: The same reporting relationship names two managers.

snippetA: The Fable app's launch market is Canada.
snippetB: The Fable app's launch market is Portugal.
rationale: The launch market is stated as two different countries.

snippetA: My legal surname is Adeyemi.
snippetB: My legal surname is Carver.
rationale: This could be an identity change, but no timing or replacement language is present.

snippetA: In 2022 I lived in Verona.
snippetB: Since 2025 I live in Tallinn.
rationale: The later residence naturally replaces the earlier one over time.

snippetA: I used to go by Mira at work.
snippetB: I now go by Milo at work.
rationale: The second memory explicitly updates a first-person identity label.

snippetA: The Oriole budget was $12,000 in March.
snippetB: The Oriole budget is now $18,000.
rationale: The numeric change is explicitly dated as an update.

snippetA: Before the merger, Noma's title was Operations Lead.
snippetB: After the merger, Noma's title is Director of Operations.
rationale: The role changed across a named organizational event.

snippetA: My preferred pharmacy was Northgate Chemist.
snippetB: My preferred pharmacy is Lakeside Apothecary now.
rationale: The phrasing marks the second as the current replacement.

snippetA: The Finch API endpoint was /v1/search during beta.
snippetB: The Finch API endpoint is /v2/query in production.
rationale: Different lifecycle stages explain the change cleanly.

snippetA: I had a Monday therapy slot last winter.
snippetB: My therapy slot moved to Thursdays this spring.
rationale: The appointment schedule is described as moving over time.

snippetA: The Rowan subscription is inactive.
snippetB: The Rowan subscription is not active.
rationale: Both memories encode the same inactive status in different wording.

snippetA: I do not eat shellfish.
snippetB: I avoid shellfish entirely.
rationale: The second restates the practical negation of eating shellfish.

snippetA: The Quill archive has no public access.
snippetB: The Quill archive is not publicly accessible.
rationale: The memories are equivalent negative formulations.

snippetA: Nico is not on the payroll.
snippetB: Nico has never been on the payroll.
rationale: Both say Nico is absent from payroll, with the second stronger but not substantively conflicting.

snippetA: The Cedar backup job is disabled.
snippetB: The Cedar backup job is not enabled.
rationale: Disabled and not enabled are the same operational state here.

snippetA: The Lyra office is on the 7th floor.
snippetB: The Lyra office has a south-facing conference room.
rationale: These are complementary facts about different attributes of the same office.

snippetA: The Zephyr team has 24 employees.
snippetB: The Zephyr team's annual revenue is $4 million.
rationale: Headcount and revenue are different attributes that can both be true.

snippetA: My 2024 tax folder is in iCloud.
snippetB: The 2024 hiking trip was in Banff.
rationale: The pair shares only the year and concerns unrelated subjects.

snippetA: The Sparrow invoice number is 1187.
snippetB: Room 1187 at the Vireo hotel has a balcony.
rationale: The shared number is incidental and the subjects are unrelated.

snippetA: I prefer oat milk in coffee.
snippetB: My coffee order uses oat milk.
rationale: These are near-duplicates of the same preference.

snippetA: The Atlas demo is scheduled for Friday.
snippetB: The Atlas demo needs a Spanish-language slide deck.
rationale: Schedule and preparation requirement are compatible facts.

snippetA: Mina's dog is named Toast.
snippetB: Mina's dog is a greyhound.
rationale: Name and breed are different compatible attributes.

snippetA: The Helio grant total is $75,000.
snippetB: The Helio grant has three reporting milestones.
rationale: Amount and milestone count are not competing values.

snippetA: I renewed my library card in May.
snippetB: May Tan is the contact for the Opal printer lease.
rationale: The shared token May has different meanings in unrelated memories.

snippetA: The Sable roadmap includes offline mode.
snippetB: The Sable roadmap includes shared folders.
rationale: A roadmap can include both features at once.

snippetA: My apartment key code starts with 42.
snippetB: The Wren survey received 42 responses.
rationale: The shared number does not make the memories related.

snippetA: The Lark cafe opens at 8 AM.
snippetB: The Lark cafe closes at 6 PM.
rationale: Opening and closing times are complementary schedule facts.

snippetA: I saved the marina receipt in the travel folder.
snippetB: The Marina account renewal is due next month.
rationale: Marina appears as a common noun in one memory and a name in another.

snippetA: The Oboe client prefers weekly status emails.
snippetB: The Oboe client prefers concise status emails.
rationale: Frequency and style preferences can both be true.

snippetA: The Peregrine model scored 81 percent on recall.
snippetB: The Peregrine model scored 81 percent on precision.
rationale: The same number applies to different metrics, so there is no contradiction.
